---
title: "ProSIFT -- Handoff File"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float:
      collapsed: true
      smooth_scroll: true
    toc_depth: 3
    theme: default
---

# ProSIFT -- Handoff File

**Last Updated:** 2026-03-27 (evening)

**Purpose:** Operational coordination across Claude sessions. Tracks current development state, active tasks, blockers, and environment details. Updated every session.

**Key principle:** The Project Documentation (ProSIFT_project_documentation.Rmd) is the architectural reference. This handoff is purely operational -- it tracks *what is happening now*, not *why things are designed the way they are*. Reference the Project Documentation for explanations rather than restating them.

---

### DOCUMENT MAINTENANCE RULES:
- **ADD** new information to appropriate sections; do not remove existing content unless archiving
- **EDIT** only when specific information needs correction
- **ARCHIVE** session log entries older than 4-6 weeks once their content has been captured in the Project Documentation
- **REFERENCE** the Project Documentation for design rationale (format: [See Project Documentation Section X.X: Title])

---

# 1. Instance Instructions

## 1.1 Instructions for Claude Code

### Session Protocol

**AT SESSION START:**

- Read the Project Documentation (if instructed) and this Handoff File
- Check "Current Development State" (Section 2) for module status overview
- Check "Pending Tasks" (Section 4.1) for assigned work items
- Review the File System Map (Section 7) to orient yourself to the project structure
- Check Computational Environment (Section 8) for environment setup
- Review Code and Style Preferences (Section 9) before writing any code

**DURING THE SESSION:**

- Follow the QC check protocol below -- pause between major steps, report metrics, and ask before proceeding
- When writing scripts, use the header format and style conventions from Section 9
- When referencing architectural decisions or design rationale, check the Project Documentation or ask the user to provide it if deeper context is needed
- When implementing cross-module interfaces, check "Integration Points" (Section 6) for data contracts
- If a module's expected inputs or outputs change during implementation, update Section 6 immediately

**AT SESSION END:**

- Update Session Log (Section 3) with a dated entry of work done, including:
  - Scripts created or modified (with file paths)
  - Output files generated (with file paths)
  - Tests run and their outcomes
  - Any warnings, unexpected results, or concerns
- Update "Current Development State" (Section 2) if module statuses changed
- Move completed tasks to "Completed Tasks" (Section 4.3) with date
- Update File System Map (Section 7) if files were created, moved, or deleted
- Flag any issues or questions in Section 5
- If a significant design decision was made, note it here and flag it for promotion to the Project Documentation Decision Register

### QC Check Protocol (Mandatory)

- Pause between major implementation steps to verify outputs before proceeding
- Report QC metrics after each step -- do not continue silently
- Ask the user if results look reasonable before moving to the next part
- If any test fails or produces unexpected results, STOP and report

**Check-in format after major steps:**
```
Step [X] complete.
  Results:
  - Output file: [path]
  - [Key metric 1]: [value] (expected: [range])
  - [Key metric 2]: [value] (expected: [range])
  QC Status: [PASS / WARNING / FAIL]
  [Notes or concerns]
  Ready to proceed to Step [Y]. Continue? (y/n)
```

**Red flags to watch for and report:**

- Tests that pass when they should fail (or vice versa)
- Unexpected file sizes, row counts, or data dimensions
- Schema mismatches between modules (check Section 6)
- Warnings from statistical or data processing functions
- Discrepancies between what was specified and what the data supports
- Silent data loss (rows/columns dropped without explanation)

---

## 1.2 Instructions for Claude.ai

### Session Protocol

**AT SESSION START:**

- Read the Project Documentation (if instructed) and this Handoff File
- Review recent Session Log entries (Section 3) to understand what has been done since your last session
- Check "Open Questions" (Section 5) for items that may need attention

**DURING THE SESSION:**

- When making significant design or architectural decisions, document them using the Decision Register format in Project Documentation Section 9
- When drafting implementation tasks, write them as actionable entries with clear acceptance criteria for the Pending Tasks queue (Section 4.1)
- When reviewing code or implementation approaches, cross-reference with the Project Documentation for architectural consistency

**AT SESSION END:**

- Promote Session Log content to the Project Documentation as appropriate:
  - Significant decisions go to Project Documentation Section 9 (Decision Register)
  - Development milestones go to Project Documentation Section 10 (Development Log)
  - New limitations discovered go to Project Documentation Section 8
- Add any new tasks to "Pending Tasks" (Section 4.1)
- Flag any open questions or blockers in Section 5

### Role and Responsibilities

**Primary responsibilities:**

- Architectural decisions and design review
- Evaluating implementation approaches and tradeoffs
- Reviewing code logic and analytical methodology
- Drafting task specifications with clear acceptance criteria
- Literature review and biological context for module design (e.g., imputation method selection, enrichment analysis design)

**Quality and validation mindset:**

- Question whether implementation choices match the architectural intent in the Project Documentation
- Consider edge cases: what happens with missing data, many-to-many ID mappings, API failures
- Verify that module interfaces match the data contracts in Section 6
- Be critical of Claude Code's outputs -- check whether the approach was sound, not just whether the code ran
- Flag scope creep: is this implementation adding complexity beyond what was specified?

---

# 2. Current Development State

## 2.1 Phase Overview

| Phase | Description | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Phase 1 | Core pipeline skeleton + pre-normalization QC | In progress | [See Project Documentation Section 6.1] |
| Phase 2 | Normalization, imputation, statistical analysis, enrichment + QC report assembly | Not started | [See Project Documentation Section 6.2] |
| Phase 3 | Database integration layer | Not started | [See Project Documentation Section 6.3] |
| Phase 4 | Interactive frontend | Not started | [See Project Documentation Section 6.4] |

**Current phase:** Phase 1 complete. Beginning Phase 2 (Module 03: Normalization & Imputation).

**Current focus:** Module 03 NORMALIZE process (bin/normalize.py + modules/local/normalize/main.nf, next up)

## 2.2 Module Status

Tracks implementation status of individual modules within and across phases. A module may be: Not started, In progress, Functional (works but incomplete), Complete, or Blocked.

| Module | Phase | Status | Last Touched | Blocking Issues | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Project structure / repo setup | 1 | Complete | 2026-03-26 | -- | main.nf, nextflow.config, conf/base.config, workflows/, modules/local/, bin/ all created and functional |
| nextflow.config + params.yml schema | 1 | Functional | 2026-03-26 | -- | local/pbs/conda/singularity profiles; samplesheet param replaces params_file; conda PATH workaround for macOS; cluster path needs updating before first cluster run |
| Conda environment | 1 | Complete | 2026-03-25 | -- | environment.yml finalized; prosift env exists locally; not yet created on cluster |
| Data preparation scripts | Pre-1 | Complete | 2026-03-26 | -- | All 3 scripts tested; samplesheet.csv generated; abundance_prefix bug fixed in prepare_prosift_input.py; 12 per-run input directories regenerated |
| Module 01: Input validation (Processes 4.1-4.3) | 1 | Complete | 2026-03-26 | -- | bin/validate_inputs.py implemented and tested standalone and through Nextflow |
| Module 01: Detection filter (Process 4.4) | 1 | Complete | 2026-03-26 | -- | bin/filter_proteins.py implemented and tested standalone and through Nextflow |
| Module 01: UniProt ID mapping (Processes 4.5-4.9) | 1 | Complete | 2026-03-26 | -- | bin/uniprot_mapping.py implemented; paginated JSON API; MD5 cache; 5 mapping statuses; ortholog columns null (Phase 3); tested end-to-end |
| Module 01: Missingness report (Process 4.10) | 1 | Complete | 2026-03-27 | -- | bin/missingness_report.py implemented; modules/local/missingness_report/main.nf wired; errorStrategy 'ignore'; stub run verified all 12 runs; standalone tested on CTXcyto. 4 plots: filter categories, per-sample detection (colored by group), missingness heatmap (filtered proteins only), per-protein missingness histogram. |
| Module 02: Pre-normalization QC/EDA | 1 | Complete | 2026-03-27 | -- | bin/prenorm_qc.py implemented and tested standalone; PRENORM_QC process wired; stub run verified all 12 runs. CTXcyto benchmark: 8,403 proteins, 6 samples, CTXcyto_KO-2 flagged on 2 criteria (extreme_median + pca_outlier). |
| Module 03: Normalization & Imputation | 2 | Not started | 2026-03-27 | -- | Design complete 2026-03-27 (03_normalization_imputation.Rmd, 572 lines). Two processes: NORMALIZE (log2, norm, diagnostics, CV) and IMPUTE (MNAR/MAR classification, MinProb/KNN, diagnostics). VSN via rpy2. DEP-style mixed imputation default. 10 params. Replaces separate Module 03 (Normalization) and Module 04 (Imputation). |
| QC Report Assembly | 2 | Not started | -- | -- | Collects diagnostic outputs from Modules 02 and 03 into consolidated HTML report. Checkpoint before differential abundance. |
| Module 04: Differential abundance (DEqMS) | 2 | Not started | -- | -- | Renumbered from Module 05. Includes volcano and MA plots. |
| Module 05: ORA / GSEA enrichment | 2 | Not started | -- | -- | Renumbered from Module 06. Includes dot plots, GSEA running score plots. |
| Module 06: Database queries (all 5 databases) | 3 | Not started | -- | -- | Renumbered from Module 07. Caching/rate-limiting framework, UniProt, PubMed, DisGeNET, DGIdb, CTD. |
| Module 07: Results assembly (SQLite) | 3 | Not started | -- | -- | Renumbered from Module 08. Replaces JSON profiles. |
| Module 08: Interactive frontend | 4 | Not started | -- | Tech selection deferred | Renumbered from Module 09. |
| Singularity definition | 1 | Not started | -- | -- | Dependency list now established (environment.yml); ready to write .def file |

## 2.3 Nextflow Process Status

Tracks which Nextflow processes exist and whether they are functional. Populated as processes are implemented.

| Process Name | Module | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| VALIDATE_INPUTS | 01 Input Validation | Functional | modules/local/validate_inputs/main.nf wired; calls bin/validate_inputs.py; tested end-to-end on all 12 runs |
| FILTER_PROTEINS | 01 Input Validation | Functional | modules/local/filter_proteins/main.nf wired; calls bin/filter_proteins.py; tested end-to-end on all 12 runs |
| UNIPROT_MAPPING | 01 Input Validation | Functional | modules/local/uniprot_mapping/main.nf wired; calls bin/uniprot_mapping.py; cache dir passed as params.outdir/cache/id_mapping; tested end-to-end on CTXcyto run |
| PRENORM_QC | 02 Pre-norm QC/EDA | Functional | modules/local/prenorm_qc/main.nf wired; calls bin/prenorm_qc.py; 17 outputs (4 data files, 5 plot pairs, 1 report); publishes to {run_id}/qc/; stub verified all 12 runs; standalone tested on CTXcyto |
| MISSINGNESS_REPORT | 01 Input Validation | Functional | modules/local/missingness_report/main.nf wired; calls bin/missingness_report.py; errorStrategy 'ignore'; optional outputs; publishDir to {run_id}/validation/; stub verified all 12 runs; standalone tested on CTXcyto. Abundance prefix bug fixed (strips abundance_ when matching columns to metadata). |
| NORMALIZE | 03 Normalization & Imputation | Not started | Needs: modules/local/normalize/main.nf, bin/normalize.py. bin/prosift_plot_utils.py is already implemented (2026-03-27). Inputs: filtered_matrix.parquet (from FILTER_PROTEINS), validated_metadata.parquet (from VALIDATE_INPUTS), params.yml. Outputs: normalized_matrix.parquet, cv_summary, 3 diagnostic plot pairs (PNG + HTML), PCA/correlation parquets, normalization summary text. VSN path requires rpy2 + Bioconductor vsn (installed: 3.78.1). |
| IMPUTE | 03 Normalization & Imputation | Not started | Added 2026-03-27. Needs: modules/local/impute/main.nf, bin/impute.py. Inputs: normalized_matrix.parquet (from NORMALIZE), validated_metadata.parquet, detection_filter_table.csv (from FILTER_PROTEINS, for MNAR/MAR classification), params.yml. Outputs: imputed_matrix.parquet (no NaNs), imputation_summary, 2 diagnostic plot types (PNG + HTML). |

---

# 3. Session Log

Maintained by Claude Code. Claude.ai reads these entries to understand what work has been done and promotes substantive content to the Project Documentation (decisions to Section 9, milestones to Section 10).

> **Entry template:**
>
> ### [DATE] ([Claude Code / Claude.ai])
> - [What was done]
> - [Key outputs generated with file paths]
> - [Tests run and outcomes]
> - [Decisions made -- flag for promotion to Project Documentation Decision Register if significant]
> - [Issues encountered or flagged]

### 2026-03-27 (Claude Code) -- Phase 1 Complete: MISSINGNESS_REPORT, Workflow Cleanup, Shared Plot Utilities

- **MISSINGNESS_REPORT (Module 01, Process 4.10) implemented and tested:**
  - `bin/missingness_report.py`: 4 interactive Plotly plots -- filter category bar chart, per-sample detection bar chart (colored by group), missingness heatmap (filtered-out proteins only, sorted by category then detection count), per-protein missingness histogram (severity-colored bars). Outputs: `{run_id}.missingness_report.html` (embedded Plotly) + `{run_id}.missingness_plots/` (4 static PNGs via kaleido).
  - Key implementation note: abundance columns carry `abundance_` prefix in the matrix but metadata `sample_id` values are bare. Fixed in both `plot_per_sample_detection` and `plot_missingness_heatmap` using `str.removeprefix("abundance_")`.
  - `modules/local/missingness_report/main.nf`: `errorStrategy 'ignore'`; both outputs `optional: true`; publishDir to `{run_id}/validation/`.
  - `workflows/prosift.nf` updated: added MISSINGNESS_REPORT include and channel wiring (joins FILTER_PROTEINS.out.filter_table + VALIDATE_INPUTS.out.matrix + VALIDATE_INPUTS.out.metadata).
  - Stub run verified all 12 runs. Standalone tested on CTXcyto.

- **Module 02 validation / spot-checks:**
  - Verified CTXcyto benchmark values against raw data: 8,403 proteins (correct), medians 23.0-23.2 log2 (consistent, appropriate for raw DIA-NN data), skewness -0.06 to -0.11 (near-zero, well-behaved), PC1 51.7% / PC2 29.6% (WT and KO separate on PC1 as expected), within-group Pearson r 0.99+, between-group 0.977-0.980.
  - Confirmed `flag_extreme_median: 2 flagged` is correct (one per group, not global); `flag_low_detection: 0` is correct (44-protein detection gap < 168-protein 2% threshold); CTXcyto_KO-2 with 2 flags (extreme_median + pca_outlier) matches expected benchmark.

- **workflows/prosift.nf multiMap cleanup:**
  - Removed unnecessary multiMap blocks for `VALIDATE_INPUTS.out.matrix` and `VALIDATE_INPUTS.out.metadata`. Confirmed via real-data test that Nextflow DSL2 process output channels are broadcast -- multiple downstream joins each receive the full stream without multiMap. `ch_input` multiMap retained (it IS a queue channel).
  - Updated ch_input comment to document the broadcast vs. queue channel distinction for future reference.

- **bin/prosift_plot_utils.py created; bin/prenorm_qc.py refactored:**
  - Extracted `plot_intensity_boxplots`, `compute_and_plot_pca`, `compute_and_plot_correlation`, `make_color_map`, `save_plot`, `_PALETTE` into shared module.
  - Added `label` parameter (default `"Pre-Normalization"`) to all three plot functions so Module 03 can call them with `label="Post-Normalization"` without any changes to the shared code.
  - Added `note` parameter to `compute_and_plot_pca` (default `None`) for the phase-specific annotation text that appeared in Module 02's PCA plot.
  - `prenorm_qc.py` updated: removed extracted functions and unused imports (`plotly.express`, `sklearn.*`); added import from `prosift_plot_utils`; updated PCA call site to pass `note=` explicitly.
  - Full Module 02 re-run post-refactor verified: identical output (8,403 proteins, CTXcyto_KO-2 flagged, all 17 files produced).

- **Design feedback documented (Module 03 pre-implementation):**
  - VSN + NA issue: `justvsn()` behavior with NA values unresolved; likely needs `vsn2()` fit/apply split. Flagged in Section 5.2.
  - MinProb reproducibility: random draws without seed break Nextflow -resume caching. Recommend adding `imputation.random_seed` param. Flagged in Section 5.2.
  - `ch_input` multiMap will need 2 new branches (normalize_params, impute_params) for Module 03 wiring.

### 2026-03-27 (Claude.ai) -- Module 03 Recombined: Normalization & Imputation; Imputation Fully Designed

- Revisited the normalization/imputation split from earlier in the session. Concluded that separate modules added wiring complexity without meaningful benefit since they always run sequentially and CV (the only analysis between them) is a few lines of code.
- **Decision: Recombine into Module 03 (Normalization & Imputation)** with two Nextflow processes (NORMALIZE, IMPUTE). This preserves the process-level boundary for re-run caching while simplifying module structure. Downstream modules renumber (Diff Abundance -> 04, Enrichment -> 05, etc.).
- Designed imputation in full detail:
  - **DEP-style mixed imputation** as default: MNAR values (SINGLE-GROUP proteins) get MinProb, MAR values (sporadic in PASSED proteins) get KNN. Classification uses Module 01's detection_filter_table.csv directly. Works fine with low n (triplicates) because classification is rule-based, not statistical.
  - **MinProb** for MNAR: draws from low-end Gaussian parameterized by sample quantile (default 1st percentile) and scaled SD (default 0.3x)
  - **KNN** for MAR: protein-wise nearest neighbors (k=10 default), imputes from similar proteins' values
  - **Left-censored (Perseus-style)** available as single-method override
  - User override: `imputation.mode: single` forces one method for all missing values
  - Sensitivity analysis deferred to later phase
- **CV on back-transformed linear scale:** 2^log2_value, then SD/mean per protein per group. Back-transform needed because normalization produces log2 output and CV is defined for linear data. The back-transformation is exact for median/quantile, approximate for VSN.
- VSN simplified: refuses non-raw data with a clear error (removed the back-transform path from earlier design)
- Wrote combined spec: 03_normalization_imputation.Rmd (572 lines), 9 design decisions, 10 parameters
- Updated all documentation: project doc Section 3.2-3.6 restructured and renumbered, new decision register entries, dev log updated, handoff module status/process table/tasks/contracts/file system map all updated for combined module and downstream renumbering

### 2026-03-27 (Claude.ai) -- Module 03 Normalization Design Complete

- Designed Module 03 (Normalization) in full, building on the module architecture decisions from earlier in the session
- Key design decision: include VSN via rpy2, accepting that Module 03 becomes a mixed Python/R module. Rationale: VSN is the community standard (DEP default, Wistar core analysis), rpy2 is already a dependency for Module 05
- **Decision: Method-aware log2 handling (Option B).** Median/quantile receive log2 data; VSN receives raw data. Eliminates the `normalization.log_transform` parameter from the stub.
- **Decision: log2(x) not log2(x+1)** for the permanent transformation. The +1 bias matters for statistical testing at the low-abundance end.
- Four methods fully specified: median (additive shift on log2 scale, pure Python), quantile (force identical distributions, pure Python), VSN (variance-stabilizing arcsinh transformation via R's justvsn(), mixed Python/R), none (log2-only or pass-through)
- Post-normalization diagnostics: box plots, PCA, correlation heatmap mirroring Module 02 formats. Shared plotting utility module (`bin/prosift_plot_utils.py`) to ensure visual consistency.
- Edge case handling specified: VSN with log2 input (back-transform + warning), method=none still produces diagnostic plots, zero values caught and converted to NaN
- Single Nextflow process (NORMALIZE), one new param (`normalization.method`, default "median")
- Wrote complete Module 03 spec (03_normalization.Rmd): Sections 1-9, 6 design decisions documented
- Updated Project Documentation: Section 3.2 expanded, new decision register entry, new dev log entry
- Updated Handoff: Module 03 status, NORMALIZE in Nextflow process table, session log, pending task, completed task, data contracts, file system map

### 2026-03-27 (Claude Code) -- Module 02 Implementation: bin/prenorm_qc.py, PRENORM_QC Process, Workflow Wiring

- Implemented `bin/prenorm_qc.py` (Module 02, PRENORM_QC process): 6 analyses (per-sample summaries, box plots, Q-Q plot, PCA, correlation heatmap, outlier flagging), 17 outputs, consolidated HTML report
- Added `python-kaleido` to environment.yml for static PNG export from Plotly figures
- Created `modules/local/prenorm_qc/main.nf`: PRENORM_QC process with 16 named output emits; publishDir to `{run_id}/qc/`
- Updated `workflows/prosift.nf`: added `prenorm_params` multiMap branch; added `VALIDATE_INPUTS.out.metadata.multiMap {}` to branch validated metadata to both FILTER_PROTEINS and PRENORM_QC; wired PRENORM_QC channel join (filtered_matrix + validated_metadata + id_mapping + params_yml)
- **Key implementation notes:** Path.with_suffix bug discovered and fixed (with_suffix replaces last dot-separated token, so `run.sample_intensity_summary.with_suffix('.png')` gives `run.png`; fixed by string concatenation); PCA uses 7,818 complete-case proteins (585 dropped); kaleido launches Chrome headlessly for PNG rendering
- Tested standalone on CTXcyto benchmark: 8,403 proteins, 6 samples, all 17 output files produced correctly
- CTXcyto results: medians ~23 log2 (expected), skewness -0.06 to -0.11 (near zero, well-behaved), PCA PC1 51.7%/PC2 29.6%; WT and KO separate clearly on PC1; CTXcyto_KO-2 flagged on 2 criteria (flag_extreme_median + flag_pca_outlier); within-group correlations 0.99+, between-group 0.977-0.980
- Verified Nextflow stub run: all 12 runs produced 17 outputs each in `{run_id}/qc/`; no workflow errors; WARN about macOS runtime metrics is expected and harmless
- **[FOLLOW-UP NEEDED]:** Install kaleido in cluster conda env before running Module 02 on mesx.sdsu.edu (`conda install -c conda-forge python-kaleido` or `pip install kaleido` in prosift env). Kaleido requires a Chromium/Chrome browser -- verify Chrome is available or headless Chromium is accessible on cluster.

### 2026-03-27 (Claude.ai) -- Module 02 Architecture: QC/EDA Rescoped, Pipeline Modules Restructured

- Conducted systematic inventory of all QC/EDA analyses for discovery proteomics, organized by pipeline timing: pre-normalization (missingness, detection counts, total intensity, raw distributions, distributional shape), both pre- and post-normalization (PCA, sample correlation, box/density plots), post-normalization pre-imputation (CV within groups), post-imputation (imputation diagnostics), post-differential-abundance (volcano/MA plots)
- Evaluated four architectural options for Module 02: (A) thin QC module + diagnostics in each downstream module, (B) two-part QC wrapping normalization/imputation, (C) merged "Data Preparation and QC" supermodule, (D) maximum granularity with separate modules per analytical step
- **Decision: Hybrid of Options A/D with QC Report Assembly checkpoint.** Module 02 rescoped to pre-normalization QC only. Normalization and imputation become separate modules (03 and 04), each producing their own diagnostic outputs. Lightweight QC Report Assembly process sits after Module 04 as a checkpoint before differential abundance.
- Confirmed volcano plots and MA plots belong to Module 05 (Differential Abundance), not QC/EDA
- Confirmed CV belongs in Module 04 (post-normalization, pre-imputation, observed values only)
- New module numbering: 01 Input Validation [complete], 02 Pre-norm QC, 03 Normalization, 04 Imputation, QC Report Assembly, 05 Differential Abundance, 06 Enrichment, 07 Database Queries, 08 Results Assembly, 09 Frontend
- Began detailed Module 02 design. Reviewed Module 01's existing missingness outputs (detection_filter_table.csv, validation_report_part2.txt) against proposed Module 02 missingness analysis. Found significant overlap: Module 01's detection filter already removes proteins with the most informative missingness patterns (SINGLE-GROUP, SPARSE, ABSENT), leaving Module 02 with only residual 0-2 missing values per protein.
- **Decision: Missingness visualizations belong in Module 01, not Module 02.** New MISSINGNESS_REPORT process (Process 4.10) added to Module 01 as a separate Nextflow process with its own script (`bin/missingness_report.py`). Separate from `filter_proteins.py` to keep filtering script lean (no matplotlib/plotly dependency).
- Four missingness plots specified: (1) filter category bar chart, (2) per-sample detection bar chart colored by group, (3) missingness heatmap for filtered-out proteins only (Option A -- ~400-500 proteins, manageable size, where the interesting patterns are), (4) per-protein missingness rate histogram across full dataset
- Module 02 scope confirmed as excluding missingness. Remaining Module 02 analyses: per-sample intensity summaries, intensity distributions, distributional shape assessment, PCA, sample correlation heatmap, outlier detection
- Updated Project Documentation: new decision register entries (module architecture, missingness in Module 01), updated Module 02 exclusion list, dev log updated
- Updated Module 01 spec: new Process 4.10 with full specification, updated outputs section, new decision register entry, new change history entry
- Updated Handoff: module status table (new MISSINGNESS_REPORT row, updated Module 02 scope), Nextflow process table, session log, pending tasks
- **[FOLLOW-UP NEEDED]:** Rename/split module doc files to match new numbering (03_normalization_imputation.Rmd -> 03_normalization.Rmd + 04_imputation.Rmd; renumber 04-08 -> 05-09; add QC report assembly doc)

### 2026-03-26 (Claude.ai) -- Module 01 Review; Architecture Decisions; Usability Assessment

- Reviewed Claude Code's Module 01 implementation: `validate_inputs.py` (Processes 4.1-4.3), `filter_proteins.py` (Process 4.4), `uniprot_mapping.py` (Processes 4.5-4.9), samplesheet-based Nextflow wiring, `accession_redirected` mapping status addition
- Walked through all Nextflow files line-by-line with developer: `main.nf`, `workflows/prosift.nf` (samplesheet channel, multiMap, join), `nextflow.config` (profiles, conda workaround, tracing), `conf/base.config` (error strategy), all three process module files (publishDir patterns, tuple I/O, stub blocks)
- Evaluated four approaches for UniProt mapping architecture (accept redundancy, pre-fork mapping, file-based locking, simple Python cache). **Decision: Option 1 (accept redundancy).** 12 parallel API calls at ~10-30 sec each is negligible cost; architectural simplicity of self-contained runs outweighs the minor waste.
- **Decision: Defer ortholog mapping to Phase 3.** Columns present but null in mapping table. Ortholog resource (Ensembl BioMart vs. JAX MGI) still an open question.
- Compiled complete Module 01 input/output summary (6 output files per run, 2 primary data artifacts for downstream consumption: filtered_matrix.parquet and id_mapping.parquet)
- **Usability assessment:** Current input workflow requires 3 preprocessing scripts + samplesheet + params files before pipeline runs. Acceptable for development phase; not suitable for bench scientists. Key finding: usability layer sits entirely outside the pipeline and can be rewritten at any point without touching Nextflow files or Python scripts. The input format contract (CSV conventions, metadata structure, params schema) is the stable interface -- everything above it is interchangeable. Added to deferred considerations.
- All Module 01 statuses updated to Complete. Ready for Module 02 design.

### 2026-03-26 (Claude Code) -- ID Mapping: accession_redirected Status Added

- Added fifth `mapping_status` value: `accession_redirected`. Covers proteins where the input accession is a secondary or merged accession in UniProt -- the API returns a different canonical than what was submitted. Previously these were silently filed as "mapped" with no note, and `input_id != uniprot_accession` with no indication of the discrepancy.
- Detection: in `parse_json_results`, after the isoform check, `is_redirected = not is_isoform and accession != input_id`. Notes field records the old -> new accession.
- CTXcyto results: 6 proteins (0.1%) reclassified from "mapped" to "accession_redirected". All 6 listed explicitly in the report.
- Report updated: new "Accession-Redirected Protein IDs" block lists each redirect pair (`input_id -> uniprot_accession`). Explains the FASTA vintage as likely cause.
- Spec updated: `01_input_validation.Rmd` Sections 4.6 (edge cases) and 4.8 (schema) updated to include the new status and its definition.
- **[FOLLOW-UP NEEDED]:** Cache stores the parsed DataFrame, not the raw API results. If parsing logic changes again (e.g., a new status added), stale cache entries will serve the old classification. A schema version field in the cache metadata JSON would prevent silent misclassification on re-runs. Not urgent now but relevant before Phase 3 changes.
- **Downstream note for future modules:** The 6 redirected proteins have `input_id != uniprot_accession`. Joins from abundance data back to the mapping table must use `input_id` as the key, not `uniprot_accession`. This is already the intended design but worth enforcing explicitly in Module 02+ implementations.

### 2026-03-26 (Claude Code) -- Module 01 Complete: bin/uniprot_mapping.py and UNIPROT_MAPPING Process

- Implemented `bin/uniprot_mapping.py` (Processes 4.5-4.9): UniProt bulk ID mapping, edge case handling, cache management, ortholog stub columns.
- **API approach:** The UniProt ID mapping API v2 (`to=UniProtKB`) does NOT support the generic TSV stream endpoint (`/idmapping/stream/{jobId}`). On completion, the status endpoint redirects to `/idmapping/uniprotkb/results/{jobId}`, which returns paginated JSON. The script paginates via `Link: <url>; rel="next"` headers (500 results/page). 17 pages for the CTXcyto run (8,403 proteins).
- **Field extraction from JSON:** `to.genes[0].geneName.value` for gene symbol; first `uniProtKBCrossReferences` entry with `database == "GeneID"` for Entrez ID; first `database == "Ensembl"` entry's `properties[key=="GeneId"].value` for Ensembl gene ID.
- **Isoform detection:** Input IDs matching `XXXXX-N` pattern (UniProt isoform suffix) that map to the canonical accession are flagged as `isoform_collapsed`. CTXcyto run: 425 isoforms (5.1%) collapsed.
- **Cache:** MD5 hash of sorted protein IDs + source type + organism; JSON metadata file records timestamp; configurable `databases.cache_days` lifetime. Cache stored at `{params.outdir}/cache/id_mapping/` (outside Nextflow work dir, intentionally -- persists across pipeline re-runs).
- **Nextflow wiring:** Rewrote `modules/local/uniprot_mapping/main.nf` (stub -> functional); added `map_params` branch to `multiMap` in `workflows/prosift.nf` (the `filter_params` branch was already consumed by the FILTER_PROTEINS join and could not be reused). Cache dir passed as `${params.outdir}/cache/id_mapping`.
- **CTXcyto results:** 8,403 proteins submitted; 7,978 mapped (94.9%), 425 isoform_collapsed (5.1%), 0 unmapped; gene symbol coverage 8,383/8,403 (99.8%), Entrez ID 8,104/8,403 (96.4%), Ensembl gene 8,119/8,403 (96.6%).
- **Tests:** script tested standalone (cache miss, then cache hit); full end-to-end pipeline run on CTXcyto run via `-resume` -- all 3 processes (VALIDATE_INPUTS, FILTER_PROTEINS, UNIPROT_MAPPING) completed successfully. Results: `results_test/CTXcyto_WT_vs_CTXcyto_KO/validation/` contains 6 files totaling ~1.1MB.
- **[FOLLOW-UP NEEDED]:** Wire UNIPROT_MAPPING cache to a stable location that survives cluster job restarts. Currently `{params.outdir}/cache/id_mapping` -- confirm this is on a persistent filesystem (not scratch) on mesx.sdsu.edu before first cluster run.
- Module 01 is now fully implemented and functional through Nextflow for all Processes 4.1-4.9.

### 2026-03-26 (Claude.ai) -- Nextflow Walkthrough; UniProt Mapping Architecture Decision

- Walked through all Nextflow files with the developer: `main.nf` (entry point), `workflows/prosift.nf` (samplesheet channel, multiMap, join operators), `nextflow.config` (params, profiles, execution tracing), `conf/base.config` (resource defaults, error strategy), `modules/local/validate_inputs/main.nf` (publishDir pattern filtering, input/output tuples, script/stub blocks), `modules/local/filter_proteins/main.nf` (publishDir without pattern, three diagnostic outputs), `modules/local/uniprot_mapping/main.nf` (stub structure)
- Key concepts covered: DSL2 imports, channels as conveyor belts, `.splitCsv()` and `.map()` operators, `meta` map pattern for tracking run identity through channels, `.multiMap()` for forking channels, `.join()` for reassembling by meta key, `.combine()` for distributing shared resources to all runs, `publishDir` with and without `pattern:` filter, `stub:` blocks for wiring tests, the `work/` directory and hash-named subdirectories, `-resume` for picking up after failures, execution trace/timeline/report outputs
- Discussed UniProt mapping architecture -- four approaches evaluated:
  - **Option 1: Accept redundancy** (each run queries UniProt independently). Simplest, no architectural changes, self-contained runs. 12 nearly-identical API calls but each takes 10-30 seconds -- negligible cost.
  - **Option 2: Pre-fork mapping** (run once on master file, distribute via `.combine()`). One API call, guaranteed consistency, but adds architectural complexity, creates a bottleneck process, and requires a new global parameter.
  - **Option 3: File-based locking** (shared cache with lock files). Fragile, race conditions, breaks Nextflow's isolation model.
  - **Option 4: Simple Python-level cache** (check for cached file, no locks). Pragmatic but non-deterministic, shared mutable state outside Nextflow's tracking.
- **Decision: Option 1 (accept redundancy).** UniProt's bulk API is fast and generous. 12 calls at this scale is ~2-4 minutes total. The architectural simplicity of keeping runs self-contained outweighs the minor API waste. If this ever becomes a bottleneck, upgrading to Option 4 (add a cache check to the Python script) requires no Nextflow changes.
- **Decision: Defer ortholog mapping to Phase 3.** The mapping table schema includes `human_ortholog_symbol`, `human_ortholog_entrez`, and `ortholog_mapping_status` columns, but they will be null for now. These columns are not consumed until Phase 3 (database queries to DGIdb and DisGeNET). The ortholog mapping resource (Ensembl BioMart vs. JAX MGI) is still an open design question that doesn't need to be resolved for Phase 1.

### 2026-03-26 (Claude Code) -- Module 01 Implementation (Processes 4.1-4.4); Nextflow Wiring

- Fixed bug in `prepare_prosift_input.py`: `abundance_prefix` was hardcoded as `""` in the params template -- leftover from when per-run abundance CSVs had bare column names. Now correctly inherited from `run_config.yml` source config (value: `"abundance_"` for CTX dataset). Regenerated all 12 params.yml files.
- Implemented `bin/validate_inputs.py` (Processes 4.1-4.3): validates abundance matrix, validates metadata, cross-validates sample IDs. Outputs `*.validated_matrix.parquet`, `*.validated_metadata.parquet`, `*.validation_report_part1.txt`. Both scripts in `bin/` are executable with `#!/usr/bin/env python3` shebangs.
- Implemented `bin/filter_proteins.py` (Process 4.4): classifies proteins as PASSED/SINGLE-GROUP/SPARSE/ABSENT, applies detection filter, produces missingness overview and detection filter summary blocks in the report. Outputs `*.filtered_matrix.parquet`, `*.detection_filter_table.csv`, `*.validation_report_part2.txt`. Fixed a bug where per-group detection rates used `sum(axis=1)` (per-protein replicate counts) instead of `sum(axis=0)` (per-sample protein counts).
- Report design: two partial reports concatenated at end. Part 1 (validate_inputs.py) covers Processes 4.1-4.3. Part 2 (filter_proteins.py) covers Process 4.4 with self-contained category definitions and missingness overview.
- Wired Nextflow processes: uncommented `modules/local/validate_inputs/main.nf` and `modules/local/filter_proteins/main.nf`; fully wired `workflows/prosift.nf` with channel logic (samplesheet -> multiMap -> VALIDATE_INPUTS -> join -> FILTER_PROTEINS). Updated `nextflow.config`: replaced `params_file` with `samplesheet` param.
- Generated `prosift_inputs/samplesheet.csv` listing all 12 runs with absolute paths to abundance, metadata, and params files.
- Resolved conda activation issue: Nextflow's `source activate` fails in non-interactive subprocesses on macOS because conda shell hooks aren't initialized. Workaround: `process.beforeScript` in the conda profile prepends the prosift env bin to PATH directly. [FOLLOW-UP NEEDED]: update the hardcoded local path (`/Users/reina/miniconda3/envs/prosift/bin`) to cluster path (`/home2/rhastings/miniconda3/envs/prosift/bin`) before first cluster run.
- Tested full pipeline end-to-end: all 12 runs completed in parallel via `nextflow run main.nf -profile local,conda --samplesheet prosift_inputs/samplesheet.csv`. Results written to `results/{run_id}/validation/` (4 files per run: filtered_matrix.parquet, detection_filter_table.csv, validation_report_part1.txt, validation_report_part2.txt).
- Results directory structure established: `results/{run_id}/validation/` for Module 01 outputs; `results/pipeline_info/` for Nextflow execution trace and reports.
- [FOLLOW-UP NEEDED]: Add samplesheet.csv generation to `prepare_prosift_input.py` so it regenerates automatically with correct absolute paths on each machine.

### 2026-03-25 (Claude Code) -- Cluster Survey; Environment Finalized

- Surveyed mesx.sdsu.edu via SSH: Oracle Linux 8.10, x86_64; conda 25.1.1; Python 3.12.9 in base; Torque 6 confirmed at `/usr/local/torque6/bin` (already on PATH -- earlier "not found" was a false negative from `find` not reaching that directory); Java/Nextflow/R/Singularity not installed
- Finalized `environment.yml`: added rpy2, r-base>=4.3, bioconductor-limma, bioconductor-deqms, gseapy, tqdm, pytest-cov; pinned pandas>=2.0,<3, numpy>=1.26,<2, scipy>=1.12, rpy2>=3.5, r-base>=4.3; updated Python pin from 3.11 to 3.12 to match cluster base
- Corrected home directory in handoff (was `/usr/scratch2/userdata2/rhastings`, correct is `/home2/rhastings`; scratch is a separate mount)
- Updated module status table: project structure and nextflow.config marked Complete/Functional; Conda environment row updated; Singularity row unblocked (dependency list now established)
- Updated Section 8.3 with finalized dependency table, cluster setup sequence, and bioconductor-deqms follow-up flag

### 2026-03-25 (Claude Code) -- Nextflow Skeleton; Parameter Rename

- Created Nextflow pipeline skeleton following nf-core conventions: `main.nf`, `nextflow.config` (params + profiles: local/pbs/conda/singularity), `conf/base.config` (default resource allocations), `workflows/prosift.nf` (top-level workflow stub), three process stubs in `modules/local/` (VALIDATE_INPUTS, FILTER_PROTEINS, UNIPROT_MAPPING), and empty `bin/` directory
- Renamed detection filter parameter from `qc.min_detection_fraction` (float, 0.67) to `qc.min_detections_per_group` (integer, 2) across all files: 12 `prosift_inputs/**/*_params.yml`, `scripts/prepare_prosift_input.py` (params template), `project_documentation/modules/01_input_validation.Rmd` (filter logic, parameter table, validation report format, category definitions, test cases, 2026-03-23 decision register entry), `project_documentation/ProSIFT_project_documentation.Rmd` (2026-03-23 decision register entry + dev log)
- Added new decision register entry in 01_input_validation.Rmd (2026-03-25: "Detection Filter Parameter Changed from Fraction to Integer") documenting the edge case and rationale
- Updated handoff file system map to reflect new Nextflow directory structure and corrected per-run directory listing (2 files per run, no abundance CSV)
- Cleared Section 4.2 parameter rename item; moved to Section 4.3

### 2026-03-25 (Claude.ai) -- Review of Claude Code Output; Simplification Decisions

- Reviewed Claude Code's data preparation work (3 scripts, 12 runs, file system restructure)
- Identified module doc version problem: Claude Code's `01_input_validation.Rmd` is the pre-session 270-line version, missing all 2026-03-23 updates. Flagged for replacement (see Section 4.2).
- Noted Claude Code added a third script (`prepare_ctx_data.py`) not in original spec -- separates DIA-NN-specific parsing from generic run definition/splitting. Reasonable but a departure from the two-script design.
- Noted 12 runs generated (4 genotype + 4 fraction + 4 region) vs. the 4 genotype runs we specified. Extra runs not harmful but weren't planned.
- Decision: simplify to shared master abundance file, eliminate per-run abundance CSVs. Cross-validation already subsets by metadata. Per-run metadata files are what define a "run."
- Decision: missingness overview with category definitions in validation report. Designed the report format with explicit definitions for PASSED, SINGLE-GROUP, SPARSE, ABSENT so the report is self-contained.
- Resolved the tools survey open question (completed by Claude Code).

### 2026-03-25 (Claude Code) -- Module 01 Spec Finalized; Section 4.2 Items Resolved

- Verified `01_input_validation.Rmd` is at 778 lines (full updated version with detection filter, peptide count support, data prep scripts, 10+ decision register entries); CRITICAL flag from 4.2 resolved
- Updated Process 4.4 output spec in Module 01 doc: replaced bulleted overview with full self-contained report format including explicit PASSED/SINGLE-GROUP/SPARSE/ABSENT category definitions and pre-filter missingness overview with per-sample detection rates
- Cleared all three Section 4.2 items; all moved to Section 4.3 completed tasks

### 2026-03-25 (Claude Code) -- Data Preparation Complete; Module 01 Design Refinement

- Completed and tested all three data preparation scripts: `prepare_ctx_data.py`, `generate_run_config.py`, `prepare_prosift_input.py`
- `prepare_prosift_input.py`: ran successfully against `run_config.yml`; produced 12 run directories, each with `_abundance.csv`, `_metadata.csv`, `_params.yml`; spot-checked abundance columns, metadata group assignments, protein counts (8,948 per run), and params.yml content -- all correct
- Data preparation layer is complete; per-run inputs are ready for Module 01 implementation
- Design decision: missingness overview added to Module 01 validation report output (Section 4.4). Rationale: detection filtering runs in Module 01 before QC/EDA, so Module 02 never sees the pre-filter data state. The validation report will capture total proteins, absent/sparse/single-group/passed counts, and per-group detection rates to document what was removed and why. Decision recorded in Module 01 Decision Register (2026-03-25).
- Merged duplicate handoff and project documentation files (copies had diverged between project_documentation/ and project_documentation/modules/); canonical copies now in project_documentation/ only

### 2026-03-23 (Claude.ai) -- Input Validation Design and Data Preparation Planning

- Inspected CTX/Synaptosome Filtered Proteins CSV (8,948 proteins, 178 columns) to inform Module 01 input spec
- Key data findings: Protein Group column has semicolon-delimited UniProt accessions (90.1% single, 9.9% multi-member groups, zero duplicate first accessions); Genes column has 240 duplicate gene symbols across 518 rows (5.8%) making gene symbols unsuitable as unique IDs; per-run missingness of 1.9-4.7% when subsetting to 6-sample runs
- Analyzed statistical tradeoffs of separate vs. combined runs for the 2x2x2 factorial design; decided on 4 separate runs
- Dropped `input.protein_id_type` parameter (hardcoded UniProt for Phase 1); retained `input.abundance_type`; added peptide count support, detection filter, column prefix parameters
- Designed two-script data preparation architecture: `generate_run_config.py` (interactive) + `prepare_prosift_input.py` (extraction)
- Updated Module 01 doc (01_input_validation.Rmd): 270 to 757 lines, 10 decision register entries, new Section 9 (Data Preparation Scripts)
- Updated master Project Documentation: Section 2.3 (input spec), Section 2.6 (ID mapping), Section 7 (CTX benchmark), 4 new decision register entries, 1 new dev log entry
- Decisions made: all promoted to Project Documentation Decision Register (2026-03-23 entries)

### 2026-03-23 (Claude Code) -- Session 2

- Implemented three data preparation scripts:
  - `scripts/prepare_ctx_data.py` -- CTX/Synaptosome-specific format conversion. Reads raw DIA-NN Filtered Proteins CSV (8,948 proteins x 178 columns), outputs master ProSIFT-format files: `CTX_synaptosome_abundance.csv` (protein_id + 24 abundance_ columns + 24 peptide_count_ columns), `CTX_synaptosome_metadata.csv` (24 samples with genotype/region/fraction), `CTX_synaptosome_protein_reference.csv`
  - `scripts/generate_run_config.py` -- Interactive run definition tool. Reads master abundance + metadata files, auto-detects protein_id/abundance_/peptide_count_ columns, derives sample groups by stripping replicate suffixes, user selects pairs to compare, auto-infers group_column from metadata. Outputs `run_config.yml`
  - `scripts/prepare_prosift_input.py` -- Reads run_config.yml + master files, splits into per-run directories with {run}_abundance.csv, {run}_metadata.csv, {run}_params.yml
- Generated 12 pairwise runs for CTX dataset: 4 genotype (KO vs WT), 4 fraction (synap vs cyto), 4 region (HIP vs CTX)
- All output files validated against source data (row counts, random value spot-checks, missing value preservation, metadata correctness)
- Output location: `prosift_inputs/` with 12 run subdirectories + master files

### 2026-03-23 (Claude Code)

- Handoff file updated to reflect Nextflow (replacing all Snakemake references)
- Updated module status table: nextflow.config + params.yml schema, SQLite results database assembly, Nextflow process status tracker
- Added GitHub repository section (Section 8.3) with remote URL, workflow instructions, and commit guidelines
- Updated .gitignore: replaced .snakemake/ with .nextflow/, .nextflow.log*, work/
- Updated file system map to reflect current directory contents

### 2026-03-20 (Claude Code)

- Project documentation reviewed and handoff file created
- No code implementation yet; project is in planning stage
- Handoff structure adapted from analysis-project template to software-development format

---

# 4. Task Queue

## 4.1 Pending Tasks

> **Entry template:**
>
> ### [Task Title]
> - **Assigned to:** [Claude.ai / Claude Code]
> - **Priority:** [High / Medium / Low]
> - **Phase:** [1 / 2 / 3 / 4]
> - **Module:** [Which module this belongs to]
> - **Description:** [What needs to be done]
> - **Context/References:** [Relevant Project Documentation sections, prior session log entries]
> - **Acceptance criteria:** [How to know when it's done]
> - **Dependencies:** [Other tasks or modules that must be complete first]

### Implement Module 03: NORMALIZE and IMPUTE Processes
- **Assigned to:** Claude Code
- **Priority:** High
- **Phase:** 2
- **Module:** Module 03
- **Description:** Implement both Nextflow processes for Module 03 (Normalization & Imputation). (1) NORMALIZE process (`bin/normalize.py`): method-aware log2 + normalization (median/quantile/VSN/none), post-normalization diagnostic plots via `bin/prosift_plot_utils.py` (already implemented 2026-03-27), CV computation on back-transformed linear scale. (2) IMPUTE process (`bin/impute.py`): MNAR/MAR classification from detection_filter_table.csv, MinProb for MNAR, KNN for MAR, imputation diagnostics. `bin/prenorm_qc.py` already refactored to import from prosift_plot_utils.
- **Context/References:** Module 03 spec (`project_documentation/modules/03_normalization_imputation.Rmd`, 572 lines); Process 1 (NORMALIZE): Sections 4.0-4.6; Process 2 (IMPUTE): Sections 4.7-4.11; Design decisions: Section 5 (9 entries); Parameters: Section 6 (10 params)
- **Pre-implementation notes:** (1) VSN + missing values: justvsn() behavior with NA not addressed in spec -- likely needs vsn2() fit/apply split rather than justvsn(); resolve before VSN path. (2) MinProb random seed: add `imputation.random_seed` param (default 42) for Nextflow -resume cache stability. (3) ch_input multiMap needs 2 new branches (normalize_params, impute_params), growing to 6 total.
- **Acceptance criteria:** (1) NORMALIZE: median normalization aligns all sample medians; quantile forces identical distributions; VSN calls R via rpy2 successfully and produces approx-log2 output; "none" applies log2-only; all 3 diagnostic plots produced for all methods; CV values on linear scale (5-50% range typical). (2) IMPUTE: SINGLE-GROUP proteins classified MNAR, PASSED-with-missing classified MAR; MinProb values cluster at low end; KNN values overlap main distribution; no NaN in output; imputation diagnostics show 3 curves (observed, MNAR, MAR). (3) Both processes wired in Nextflow, stub-tested on all 12 runs, end-to-end tested on CTXcyto. (4) Edge cases: VSN with non-raw data produces clear error; zero values caught and converted to NaN.
- **Dependencies:** All Phase 1 complete (done). vsn package: installed (3.78.1). prosift_plot_utils.py: implemented.

## 4.2 Decisions and Designs for Review

> Implementation choices, design tradeoffs, or prototype outputs produced by Claude Code
> that need user or Claude.ai sign-off before building further.

None currently.

## 4.3 Completed Tasks

> **Entry format:** [Task name] -- [Date completed] -- [Output location / notes]

- Data preparation scripts (prepare_ctx_data.py, generate_run_config.py, prepare_prosift_input.py) -- 2026-03-25 -- scripts/
- CTX/Synaptosome 12-run input generation -- 2026-03-25 -- prosift_inputs/
- Simplify to shared master abundance file (eliminate per-run abundance CSVs) -- 2026-03-25 -- scripts/prepare_prosift_input.py updated; 12 per-run abundance CSVs deleted; params.yml files regenerated with ../master path
- Module 01 doc version resolved: 01_input_validation.Rmd confirmed at 778 lines with all 2026-03-23 updates -- 2026-03-25
- Missingness overview spec added to Module 01 Process 4.4 (with full report format and category definitions) -- 2026-03-25 -- project_documentation/modules/01_input_validation.Rmd
- Nextflow pipeline skeleton created (nf-core conventions) -- 2026-03-25 -- main.nf, nextflow.config, conf/base.config, workflows/prosift.nf, modules/local/{validate_inputs,filter_proteins,uniprot_mapping}/main.nf, bin/
- Parameter rename (min_detection_fraction -> min_detections_per_group) -- 2026-03-25 -- all 12 params.yml files, scripts/prepare_prosift_input.py, 01_input_validation.Rmd (logic, table, report format, category defs, decision register), ProSIFT_project_documentation.Rmd
- environment.yml finalized (Python 3.12, all Phase 1+ dependencies, version pins) -- 2026-03-25
- Cluster surveyed via SSH; confirmed Torque 6, Oracle Linux 8.10, x86_64, Python 3.12 in base -- 2026-03-25
- abundance_prefix bug fixed in prepare_prosift_input.py; all 12 params.yml regenerated with correct prefix -- 2026-03-26
- bin/validate_inputs.py implemented and tested (Processes 4.1-4.3) -- 2026-03-26
- bin/filter_proteins.py implemented and tested (Process 4.4) -- 2026-03-26
- Nextflow processes wired (VALIDATE_INPUTS, FILTER_PROTEINS); full pipeline tested end-to-end on all 12 runs -- 2026-03-26
- samplesheet.csv generated (prosift_inputs/samplesheet.csv) -- 2026-03-26
- Results directory structure established (results/{run_id}/validation/) -- 2026-03-26
- bin/uniprot_mapping.py implemented and tested (Processes 4.5-4.9); UNIPROT_MAPPING process wired -- 2026-03-26 -- bin/uniprot_mapping.py, modules/local/uniprot_mapping/main.nf, workflows/prosift.nf
- Module 01 end-to-end pipeline tested (all 3 processes, CTXcyto run): 6 output files in results_test/CTXcyto_WT_vs_CTXcyto_KO/validation/ -- 2026-03-26
- accession_redirected mapping status added to bin/uniprot_mapping.py; spec updated (01_input_validation.Rmd sections 4.6 and 4.8) -- 2026-03-26
- Module documentation renamed/split to match revised pipeline architecture -- 2026-03-27 -- split 03_normalization_imputation.Rmd -> 03_normalization.Rmd + 04_imputation.Rmd; created 04b_qc_report_assembly.Rmd; renumbered 04-08 -> 06-10 (git mv); updated 02_qc_eda.Rmd header
- Module 02 design spec finalized (02_qc_eda.Rmd -- 9 design decisions, full output schema, test criteria) -- 2026-03-27 -- Claude.ai session
- Module 02 (bin/prenorm_qc.py + PRENORM_QC process) implemented and tested -- 2026-03-27 -- 17 outputs; CTXcyto_KO-2 flagged (extreme_median + pca_outlier); standalone and stub runs verified
- Module 03 (Normalization & Imputation) design spec completed -- 2026-03-27 -- project_documentation/modules/03_normalization_imputation.Rmd (572 lines). Recombined normalization + imputation into one module with two processes (NORMALIZE, IMPUTE). Method-aware log2, 4 norm methods (median/quantile/VSN/none), DEP-style mixed imputation (MinProb MNAR + KNN MAR), CV on back-transformed linear scale, 9 design decisions, 10 parameters. Replaces earlier separate 03_normalization.Rmd. Ready for Claude Code implementation.
- Module 01 MISSINGNESS_REPORT implemented and tested -- 2026-03-27 -- bin/missingness_report.py, modules/local/missingness_report/main.nf. 4 Plotly plots (filter categories, per-sample detection colored by group, missingness heatmap for filtered-out proteins, per-protein histogram). errorStrategy 'ignore'. HTML report + static PNGs. Stub run verified all 12 runs. Standalone tested on CTXcyto.
- Module 02 validation/spot-checks -- 2026-03-27 -- Verified CTXcyto benchmark against raw data: 8,403 proteins, 6 samples, medians ~23 log2 (consistent across samples before normalization), within-group Pearson r 0.99+, CTXcyto_KO-2 flagged on 2 criteria (extreme_median + pca_outlier). All 17 output files correct.
- workflows/prosift.nf multiMap cleanup -- 2026-03-27 -- Removed unnecessary multiMap blocks for VALIDATE_INPUTS.out.matrix and VALIDATE_INPUTS.out.metadata (process output channels are broadcast in DSL2; only queue channels need multiMap). Both channels now referenced directly. Confirmed safe via real-data double-consumption test. Comment updated to document the broadcast vs. queue channel distinction.
- bin/prosift_plot_utils.py created; bin/prenorm_qc.py refactored -- 2026-03-27 -- Extracted plot_intensity_boxplots, compute_and_plot_pca, compute_and_plot_correlation, make_color_map, save_plot into shared module. Added label parameter (default "Pre-Normalization") and note parameter on PCA for phase-specific annotations. prenorm_qc.py import updated; full Module 02 run re-verified post-refactor (identical output).

---

# 5. Open Questions and Blockers

## 5.1 Blockers

> Issues that are preventing progress on a specific module or task.
>
> **Entry format:** [DATE opened] [Module affected] [Description] [Status/progress]

None currently.

## 5.2 Open Questions

> Questions that need resolution but are not blocking immediate work.
>
> **Entry format:** [DATE opened] [Question] [Status/progress]

- 2026-03-20 -- PubMed co-occurrence normalization approach not yet selected [See Project Documentation Section 4.3]
- 2026-03-20 -- Frontend technology not yet selected (deferred to after Phase 2) [See Project Documentation Section 3.7]
- ~~2026-03-26 -- nextflow.config conda profile has hardcoded local path~~ Resolved 2026-03-26: `process.beforeScript` now uses `$(conda info --base)/envs/prosift/bin` to find the conda prefix dynamically. Works on any machine where conda is on PATH and the `prosift` env exists -- no edits needed when deploying to cluster.
- ~~2026-03-26 -- samplesheet.csv generation not yet added to `prepare_prosift_input.py`~~ Resolved 2026-03-26: `write_samplesheet()` added to `prepare_prosift_input.py`; samplesheet now auto-generated with absolute paths on every run.
- 2026-03-26 -- ID mapping cache has no schema version. If parsing logic changes (new status added, field renamed), existing cache files will be loaded and served with the old classification without any warning. Adding a `schema_version` field to the cache metadata JSON would protect against this. Low priority now; becomes important before Phase 3 changes to the mapping logic.
- 2026-03-26 -- UniProt cache dir on cluster must be on persistent storage. Currently `{params.outdir}/cache/id_mapping`. Confirm `params.outdir` resolves to a persistent mount (home2 or scratch, not a temp FS) before first cluster run. If outdir is on scratch, the cache may be deleted between submissions, defeating the point.
- 2026-03-26 -- Input usability: current workflow requires 3 preprocessing scripts + samplesheet + per-run params/metadata before pipeline runs. Acceptable for development; not suitable for bench scientists. The usability layer sits entirely outside the pipeline and can be rewritten at any point without architectural changes. Deferred to post-Phase 1. [See also Project Documentation Section 6.5]
- 2026-03-27 -- Module documentation files need renaming/splitting to match revised module architecture. Not blocking (existing files are placeholders for Modules 03-08 anyway), but should be done before writing detailed specs. See Pending Tasks.
- ~~2026-03-27 -- Bioconductor `vsn` package availability in conda env.~~ Resolved 2026-03-27: `vsn` 3.78.1 installed via `BiocManager::install("vsn")` in the prosift conda env. `library(vsn)` confirmed working. [FOLLOW-UP NEEDED]: When creating the prosift env on the cluster, `vsn` will require the same BiocManager install step -- it is not installable via bioconda. Add this to cluster setup notes.
- 2026-03-27 -- VSN normalization + missing values: the spec calls `justvsn()` but this function may not handle NA values in a plain R matrix. For the CTXcyto dataset, ~585 proteins have at least one missing value. If justvsn() errors on NA, the fix is to use `vsn2()` (fits the model on the data) and then apply the fitted parameters -- which works on complete-case proteins and applies the same transformation to all. Resolve before implementing the VSN code path.
- 2026-03-27 -- MinProb imputation reproducibility: MinProb uses numpy random draws. Without a fixed seed, IMPUTE will produce different output every run, breaking Nextflow's -resume caching (output checksums won't match). Add `imputation.random_seed` (int, default 42) to the Module 03 params table and use it in impute.py.

## 5.3 Resolved Questions

> **Entry format:** **Q:** [Question] **A:** [Answer] ([Date resolved])

- **Q:** Benchmark/validation dataset not yet identified. **A:** CTX/Synaptosome dataset (Blanco-Suarez Lab, Wistar project 25-L185) selected as Phase 1 benchmark. 4 separate runs, comparison against Wistar core results. (2026-03-23) [See Project Documentation Section 7]
- **Q:** Survey of existing proteomics analysis tools needed. **A:** Completed by Claude Code (2026-03-25). Three-category survey (statistical platforms, database resources, enrichment tools) now in Project Documentation Section 1.4. (2026-03-25)

---

# 6. Integration Points

This section tracks the data contracts between modules -- what each module produces and what downstream modules expect to consume. When a module's interface changes during implementation, update this section immediately and check all affected downstream modules.

[See Project Documentation Section 2.2 for the full pipeline DAG and dependency logic.]

## 6.1 Data Contracts

Each entry defines what a module outputs and which downstream modules consume that output. Add entries here as modules are implemented, using the following template:

> ### [Producer Module] --> [Consumer Module]
> - **Producer:** [Nextflow process or module name]
> - **Consumer:** [Nextflow process or module name]
> - **Format:** [Parquet / CSV / JSON / etc.]
> - **Schema:** [Column names, types, and any constraints]
> - **Notes:** [Edge cases, relevant Project Documentation references]

[See Project Documentation Section 2.2 for the full pipeline DAG and dependency logic.]

No data contracts defined yet. Populate as modules are implemented.

### Module 01 (Input Validation & ID Mapping) --> Module 02 (Pre-Normalization QC/EDA)
- **Producer:** FILTER_PROTEINS, UNIPROT_MAPPING
- **Consumer:** Module 02 Pre-Normalization QC/EDA process(es) (not yet implemented)
- **Primary data artifacts:**
  - `{run_id}.filtered_matrix.parquet` -- post-detection-filter abundance data (~8,500-8,800 proteins x 6 samples per run, plus peptide count columns). Parquet format. Produced by FILTER_PROTEINS.
  - `{run_id}.id_mapping.parquet` -- protein ID mapping table (10 columns: protein_id, uniprot_accession, gene_symbol_mouse, entrez_id_mouse, ensembl_gene_mouse, human_ortholog_symbol [null], human_ortholog_entrez [null], ortholog_mapping_status [null], mapping_status, mapping_notes). One row per protein. Produced by UNIPROT_MAPPING.
- **Join key:** `protein_id` (UniProt accession, first from semicolon-delimited protein group). NOTE: for 6 accession-redirected proteins per run, `protein_id` (input accession) differs from `uniprot_accession` (canonical). Downstream joins must use `protein_id`, not `uniprot_accession`.
- **Also available (diagnostic, not primary):** validation_report_part1.txt, validation_report_part2.txt, validation_report_part3.txt -- published to `results/{run_id}/validation/` but not consumed by downstream processes.
- **NOTE:** `detection_filter_table.csv` was previously diagnostic-only but is now also a primary analytical input for the IMPUTE process (MNAR/MAR classification). See Module 01 -> Module 03 IMPUTE contract below.

### Module 01 (Input Validation & ID Mapping) --> Module 03 NORMALIZE
- **Producer:** FILTER_PROTEINS, VALIDATE_INPUTS
- **Consumer:** NORMALIZE (not yet implemented, Phase 2)
- **Primary data artifacts:**
  - `{run_id}.filtered_matrix.parquet` -- same as Module 02 input. The main analytical spine passes through Module 03, not Module 02 (Module 02 branches off for QC without modifying data).
  - `{run_id}.validated_metadata.parquet` -- sample-to-group assignments.
- **Note:** Module 02's outputs (sample_flags, QC report) are diagnostic and do not feed into Module 03. The data flowing to Module 03 is the same filtered matrix from Module 01.

### Module 01 (Input Validation & ID Mapping) --> Module 03 IMPUTE
- **Producer:** FILTER_PROTEINS
- **Consumer:** IMPUTE (not yet implemented, Phase 2)
- **Primary data artifact:**
  - `{run_id}.detection_filter_table.csv` -- per-protein filter status (PASSED, SINGLE-GROUP, SPARSE, ABSENT) and per-group detection counts. Used for MNAR/MAR classification: SINGLE-GROUP = MNAR, PASSED with missing values = MAR.
- **Note:** This file was previously diagnostic-only. It is now a primary analytical input because the DEP-style mixed imputation classification maps directly to Module 01's filter categories.

### Module 03 NORMALIZE --> Module 03 IMPUTE
- **Producer:** NORMALIZE (not yet implemented)
- **Consumer:** IMPUTE (not yet implemented)
- **Primary data artifacts:**
  - `{run_id}.normalized_matrix.parquet` -- approximately log2-scale, normalized abundance data. Same shape as filtered matrix. Peptide count columns passed through unchanged. Missing values remain NaN.
- **Data scale:** Always approximately log2, regardless of normalization method.
- **Join key:** `protein_id`.

### Module 03 IMPUTE --> Module 04 (Differential Abundance)
- **Producer:** IMPUTE (not yet implemented)
- **Consumer:** Module 04 Differential Abundance (not yet implemented, Phase 2)
- **Primary data artifacts:**
  - `{run_id}.imputed_matrix.parquet` -- fully imputed, approximately log2-scale, normalized. No NaN values. Peptide count columns passed through. This is the analysis-ready matrix.
- **Join key:** `protein_id`.

### Module 03 --> QC Report Assembly
- **Producer:** NORMALIZE and IMPUTE (not yet implemented)
- **Consumer:** QC Report Assembly process (not yet implemented, Phase 2)
- **Diagnostic artifacts from NORMALIZE:**
  - `{run_id}.postnorm_boxplots` (PNG + HTML)
  - `{run_id}.postnorm_pca_scatter` (PNG + HTML)
  - `{run_id}.postnorm_correlation_heatmap` (PNG + HTML)
  - `{run_id}.postnorm_pca_results.parquet`
  - `{run_id}.postnorm_correlation_matrix.parquet`
  - `{run_id}.cv_summary.parquet` + `.csv`
  - `{run_id}.normalization_summary.txt`
- **Diagnostic artifacts from IMPUTE:**
  - `{run_id}.imputation_distributions` (PNG + HTML)
  - `{run_id}.imputation_fractions` (PNG + HTML)
  - `{run_id}.imputation_summary.parquet` + `.csv`
  - `{run_id}.imputation_summary.txt`
- **Note:** Post-normalization plots mirror Module 02's pre-normalization plots for before/after comparison.

---

# 7. File System Map

## 7.1 Directory Structure

**Last updated by Claude Code:** 2026-03-27

```
ProSIFT/
│
├── .gitignore
├── CLAUDE.md                                # Project-level Claude Code instructions
├── environment.yml                          # Conda environment spec (Python 3.12, all Phase 1+ deps)
├── main.nf                                  # Nextflow entry point
├── nextflow.config                          # Pipeline config (params, profiles: local/pbs/conda/singularity)
│
├── conf/
│   └── base.config                          # Default resource allocations per process
│
├── workflows/
│   └── prosift.nf                           # Top-level PROSIFT workflow (all Phase 1 processes wired; multiMap cleanup 2026-03-27)
│
├── modules/
│   └── local/
│       ├── validate_inputs/main.nf          # Process: VALIDATE_INPUTS (functional)
│       ├── filter_proteins/main.nf          # Process: FILTER_PROTEINS (functional)
│       ├── uniprot_mapping/main.nf          # Process: UNIPROT_MAPPING (functional)
│       ├── prenorm_qc/main.nf              # Process: PRENORM_QC (functional)
│       ├── missingness_report/main.nf       # Process: MISSINGNESS_REPORT (functional)
│       ├── normalize/main.nf               # Process: NORMALIZE (not yet created)
│       └── impute/main.nf                  # Process: IMPUTE (not yet created)
│
├── bin/                                     # Python scripts called from Nextflow processes (executable, shebangs set)
│   ├── validate_inputs.py                   # Processes 4.1-4.3: matrix/metadata/cross-validation
│   ├── filter_proteins.py                   # Process 4.4: detection filter + missingness overview
│   ├── uniprot_mapping.py                   # Processes 4.5-4.9: UniProt bulk ID mapping + cache
│   ├── prenorm_qc.py                       # Module 02: pre-normalization QC/EDA (functional)
│   ├── missingness_report.py               # Process 4.10: missingness visualizations (functional, 2026-03-27)
│   ├── prosift_plot_utils.py               # Shared plotting utilities for Modules 02-03 (functional, 2026-03-27)
│   ├── normalize.py                         # Module 03 NORMALIZE: log2 + normalization + diagnostics + CV (not yet created)
│   └── impute.py                            # Module 03 IMPUTE: MNAR/MAR classification + imputation + diagnostics (not yet created)
│
├── project_documentation/
│   ├── ProSIFT_project_documentation.Rmd    # Architectural reference (Project Documentation)
│   ├── ProSIFT_handoff.Rmd                  # THIS FILE - operational coordination
│   ├── ProSIFT_handoff.html                 # Rendered handoff
│   ├── figures/
│   │   ├── prosift_pipeline_dag.svg         # Pipeline DAG diagram
│   │   └── prosift_input_validation_workflow.svg
│   └── modules/
│       ├── 01_input_validation.Rmd          # Module 01: Input Validation & ID Mapping (design complete, implemented)
│       ├── 02_qc_eda.Rmd                    # Module 02: Pre-Normalization QC/EDA (design complete, implemented)
│       ├── 03_normalization_imputation.Rmd  # Module 03: Normalization & Imputation (design complete 2026-03-27)
│       ├── 04b_qc_report_assembly.Rmd       # QC Report Assembly checkpoint (stub)
│       ├── 04_differential_abundance.Rmd    # Module 04: Differential Abundance (stub; renumbered from 05/06)
│       ├── 05_enrichment.Rmd                # Module 05: Enrichment (stub; renumbered from 06/07)
│       ├── 06_database_queries.Rmd          # Module 06: Database Queries (stub; renumbered from 07/08)
│       ├── 07_results_assembly.Rmd          # Module 07: Results Assembly (stub; renumbered from 08/09)
│       └── 08_frontend.Rmd                  # Module 08: Frontend (stub; renumbered from 09/10)
│
├── CTX_synaptosome_project_data/
│   └── Filtered Proteins-Table 1.csv        # Raw DIA-NN output (source data, not committed)
│
├── scripts/                                 # Data preparation scripts (not part of pipeline)
│   ├── prepare_ctx_data.py                  # CTX-specific: raw DIA-NN -> master ProSIFT files
│   ├── generate_run_config.py               # Interactive: master files -> run_config.yml
│   └── prepare_prosift_input.py             # run_config.yml -> per-run input directories + samplesheet.csv
│
├── prosift_inputs/                          # Pre-pipeline inputs (generated by data prep scripts)
│   ├── CTX_synaptosome_abundance.csv        # Master abundance (8,948 proteins x 49 cols; shared by all runs)
│   ├── CTX_synaptosome_metadata.csv         # Master metadata (24 samples)
│   ├── CTX_synaptosome_protein_reference.csv # Protein reference (gene symbols, descriptions)
│   ├── run_config.yml                       # 12 run definitions (generated by generate_run_config.py)
│   ├── samplesheet.csv                      # Nextflow input: one row per run (run_id, abundance, metadata, params)
│   ├── CTXcyto_WT_vs_CTXcyto_KO/           # Per-run directories (12 total, 2 files each)
│   │   ├── CTXcyto_WT_vs_CTXcyto_KO_metadata.csv   # sample_id + group column for this run's 6 samples
│   │   └── CTXcyto_WT_vs_CTXcyto_KO_params.yml     # Run params; abundance_matrix -> ../CTX_synaptosome_abundance.csv
│   └── [11 more run directories, same structure]
│
├── work/                                    # Nextflow work directory (not committed; auto-generated on run)
│
└── results/                                 # Pipeline outputs (not committed; populated on run)
    ├── cache/
    │   └── id_mapping/                      # UniProt API response cache (persists across runs; key = MD5 hash)
    ├── pipeline_info/                       # Nextflow execution trace, timeline, report
    └── {run_id}/
        ├── validation/                      # Module 01 outputs per run
        │   ├── {run_id}.filtered_matrix.parquet        # Proteins passing detection filter (input to Modules 02, 03)
        │   ├── {run_id}.detection_filter_table.csv     # Per-protein filter status and detection counts
        │   ├── {run_id}.id_mapping.parquet             # UniProt mapping table (10 cols; input to all downstream modules)
        │   ├── {run_id}.validation_report_part1.txt    # Processes 4.1-4.3 report
        │   ├── {run_id}.validation_report_part2.txt    # Process 4.4 report (filter summary + missingness)
        │   ├── {run_id}.validation_report_part3.txt    # Processes 4.5-4.9 report (mapping summary + coverage)
        │   ├── {run_id}.missingness_report.html        # Process 4.10: interactive missingness report (HTML with 4 Plotly plots)
        │   └── {run_id}.missingness_plots/             # Process 4.10: static PNGs (4 files: filter_categories, per_sample_detection, missingness_heatmap, missingness_histogram)
        ├── qc/                              # Module 02 outputs per run (functional)
        │   ├── {run_id}.sample_summary.parquet          # Per-sample metrics
        │   ├── {run_id}.sample_summary.csv
        │   ├── {run_id}.sample_flags.parquet             # Per-sample outlier flags
        │   ├── {run_id}.sample_flags.csv
        │   ├── {run_id}.correlation_matrix.parquet       # Sample-by-sample Pearson correlation
        │   ├── {run_id}.pca_results.parquet              # PCA coordinates + variance explained
        │   ├── {run_id}.prenorm_qc_report.html           # Consolidated QC report with all plots
        │   ├── {run_id}.sample_intensity_summary.png/.html
        │   ├── {run_id}.intensity_boxplots.png/.html
        │   ├── {run_id}.qq_plot.png/.html
        │   ├── {run_id}.pca_scatter.png/.html
        │   └── {run_id}.correlation_heatmap.png/.html
        └── normalization_imputation/          # Module 03 outputs per run (not yet created)
            ├── {run_id}.normalized_matrix.parquet        # Normalized data (approx log2; input to IMPUTE)
            ├── {run_id}.normalization_summary.txt        # Method used, log2 applied, warnings
            ├── {run_id}.cv_summary.parquet               # Per-protein per-group CV (linear scale)
            ├── {run_id}.cv_summary.csv
            ├── {run_id}.postnorm_pca_results.parquet
            ├── {run_id}.postnorm_correlation_matrix.parquet
            ├── {run_id}.postnorm_boxplots.png/.html
            ├── {run_id}.postnorm_pca_scatter.png/.html
            ├── {run_id}.postnorm_correlation_heatmap.png/.html
            ├── {run_id}.imputed_matrix.parquet           # Fully imputed data (no NaN; input to Module 04)
            ├── {run_id}.imputation_summary.parquet       # Per-protein imputation class and counts
            ├── {run_id}.imputation_summary.csv
            ├── {run_id}.imputation_summary.txt
            ├── {run_id}.imputation_distributions.png/.html
            └── {run_id}.imputation_fractions.png/.html
```

## 7.2 Scripts Reference

| Script | Module | Description | Last Updated |
|---|---|---|---|
| scripts/prepare_ctx_data.py | Data prep | Converts raw DIA-NN CTX output to master ProSIFT-format files | 2026-03-25 |
| scripts/generate_run_config.py | Data prep | Interactive tool: define pairwise runs from master files, outputs run_config.yml | 2026-03-25 |
| scripts/prepare_prosift_input.py | Data prep | Splits master files into per-run input directories using run_config.yml; generates samplesheet.csv | 2026-03-26 |
| bin/validate_inputs.py | Module 01 | Processes 4.1-4.3: validates abundance matrix, metadata, cross-validates samples; outputs validated Parquets + report part 1 | 2026-03-26 |
| bin/filter_proteins.py | Module 01 | Process 4.4: detection filter; classifies proteins PASSED/SINGLE-GROUP/SPARSE/ABSENT; outputs filtered Parquet, filter table, report part 2 | 2026-03-26 |
| bin/uniprot_mapping.py | Module 01 | Processes 4.5-4.9: UniProt bulk ID mapping via paginated JSON results endpoint; MD5-keyed file cache; ortholog columns null (Phase 3); outputs id_mapping.parquet + report part 3 | 2026-03-26 |
| bin/missingness_report.py | Module 01 | Process 4.10: 4 missingness plots (filter categories, per-sample detection, heatmap, histogram); HTML report + static PNGs via kaleido | 2026-03-27 |
| bin/prenorm_qc.py | Module 02 | Pre-normalization QC/EDA: per-sample summaries, box plots, Q-Q plot, PCA, correlation heatmap, outlier flagging; outputs 4 data files + 5 plot types + HTML report. Imports shared plot functions from prosift_plot_utils.py. | 2026-03-27 |
| bin/prosift_plot_utils.py | Shared | Shared plotting utilities for Modules 02 and 03: plot_intensity_boxplots, compute_and_plot_pca, compute_and_plot_correlation, make_color_map, save_plot. label and note parameters allow phase-specific titles and annotations. | 2026-03-27 |
| bin/normalize.py | Module 03 | NORMALIZE process: method-aware log2 + median/quantile/VSN/none + post-norm diagnostics + CV computation. VSN calls R via rpy2. | Not yet created |
| bin/impute.py | Module 03 | IMPUTE process: MNAR/MAR classification from detection_filter_table.csv, MinProb for MNAR, KNN for MAR, imputation diagnostics. | Not yet created |

## 7.3 File System Update Instructions

When creating, moving, or deleting files:

1. Update the directory tree above
2. Update the "Last updated" date
3. Add notes for new directories or significant files
4. Update Scripts Reference table if new scripts created
5. Check if new files need GitHub inclusion/exclusion (see Sections 8.4-8.5)

---

# 8. Computational Environment

## 8.1 Platform

**Local machine:** macOS (development, documentation, git)

**Cluster:** mesx.sdsu.edu (pipeline execution)

*Surveyed via SSH 2026-03-25. All entries below are confirmed unless noted.*

- SSH access: `ssh rhastings@mesx.sdsu.edu` (key-based auth, no password)
- OS: Oracle Linux Server 8.10, x86_64
- Home directory: `/home2/rhastings` (2.5 TB available)
- Scratch directory: `/usr/scratch2/userdata2/rhastings` (11 TB available)
- Conda: miniconda3 25.1.1 at `/home2/rhastings/miniconda3`; Python 3.12.9 in base
- Existing conda envs: `base`, `classes`, `ml_hop` -- no `prosift` env yet
- Job scheduler: **Torque 6** at `/usr/local/torque6/bin` (on PATH); `pbspro` Nextflow executor is correct
- Java: not installed system-wide; will be installed via `openjdk=17` in prosift conda env
- Nextflow: not installed; will be installed via conda
- R: not installed; will be installed via conda (r-base>=4.3)
- Singularity/Apptainer: not installed; deferred to later phase
- Git: not installed on cluster (by design -- rsync workflow, see Section 8.2)

## 8.2 Development Workflow: Local + Cluster via rsync

**Decision (2026-03-23):** Code is developed and documented locally. Files are synced to the cluster via `rsync` for execution. Git is used only for intentional milestones, not for shuttling changes between machines.

**Why this approach:** The cluster lacks git. Installing it is possible (via conda) but using git to sync between local and cluster would clutter the commit history with small "fix path" / "increase memory" commits during iterative debugging. rsync provides instant, low-friction syncing over SSH with no version control overhead. Git commits are reserved for meaningful checkpoints ("QC module working", "imputation implemented").

**Sync local to cluster (push code for execution):**

```bash
rsync -avz --exclude='.git' --exclude='data/' --exclude='results/' \
  --exclude='.nextflow/' --exclude='work/' --exclude='.DS_Store' \
  "/Users/reina/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Blanco-Suárez Lab/ProSIFT/" \
  rhastings@mesx.sdsu.edu:~/ProSIFT/
```

**Sync cluster to local (pull back bug fixes or results):**

```bash
# Pull back code changes (e.g., after fixing a bug on the cluster)
rsync -avz rhastings@mesx.sdsu.edu:~/ProSIFT/workflow/ \
  "/Users/reina/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Blanco-Suárez Lab/ProSIFT/workflow/"

# Pull back results (when needed for local inspection)
rsync -avz rhastings@mesx.sdsu.edu:~/ProSIFT/results/ \
  "/Users/reina/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Blanco-Suárez Lab/ProSIFT/results/"
```

**What does NOT get synced to the cluster:**

- `.git/` directory (git is local only)
- `data/` and `results/` (data lives on the cluster; results are pulled back selectively)
